source_review_policy: Zekelman 2025 is trusted teacher-approved standards material. Yossi review is an extraction-accuracy, Hebrew/table fidelity, classification, and mapping pass rather than a new educational approval cycle.

overview.student_expectation: Students build cumulative vocabulary and grammar knowledge in לשון הקודש so they can translate and analyze Chumash text more independently.

question_generation_relevance.notes: This is the strongest standards area for future Chumash diagnostic item design, but it remains draft source extraction only.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: This standard area most directly supports adaptive Chumash skill diagnosis.

overview.notes: The 2025 PDF is readable enough for first-pass structuring, but table layout and some Hebrew display still require manual confirmation.

substandards.student_expectation: Builds a cumulative noun vocabulary, then uses previously learned nouns for contextual translation and multiple-meaning choices.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Highly relevant to future diagnostic and question-generation lanes.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Directly assessable through word meaning, contextual meaning, and transfer into unlearned פסוקים.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.
additional_rows_summary: Gives the correct noun when given the English translation.
Groups nouns by shared שם עצם.
Moves from teacher-prompted to independent identification of learned nouns in unlearned פסוקים.
Chooses among multiple translations of a single word based on context.

substandards.student_expectation: Builds cumulative root vocabulary for verbs, then identifies weak-letter and altered-root patterns and chooses contextual translations.

question_generation_relevance.notes: One of the clearest future rule-to-question bridges in the standards.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Root meaning and root-identification skills are central to later independent Chumash reading.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.
additional_rows_summary: Provides the correct שורש when given the English meaning.
Progresses from consecutive-letter root recognition to weak-letter and altered-root recognition.
Groups verbs/adjectives that share a root.
Chooses contextual translation for roots with multiple meanings.

substandards.student_expectation: Builds a cumulative bank of function words and chooses contextual meanings for words with multiple uses.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Very relevant to later direct translation and lane-routing decisions.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Function-word confusion is a strong diagnostic signal in Chumash reading.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.
additional_rows_summary: Provides the correct Hebrew word when given the English translation.
Chooses contextual meanings for words such as כי, את, עוד, and על.

substandards.student_expectation: Moves from identifying nouns and adjectives in learned words to recognizing gender, number, irregular forms, and סמיכות in unlearned פסוקים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Strong candidate for future diagnostic grammar lanes.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Concrete, teachable, and directly connected to Chumash translation accuracy.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

core_level_expectations.3: Identify nouns in unlearned פסוקים by form
core_level_expectations.4: Handle noun forms that resemble verbs/adjectives
core_level_expectations.5: Continue noun/adjective identification in unlearned פסוקים
core_level_expectations.8: Independent noun/adjective identification in unlearned פסוקים

additional_rows_summary: Distinguishes זכר/נקבה nouns, including common irregulars.
Distinguishes singular/plural, including irregular endings and English mismatches.
Identifies adjectives by form and context.
Matches nouns and adjectives by gender/number.
Recognizes and identifies סמיכות with increasing independence.

substandards.student_expectation: Progresses from basic English and Hebrew pronoun categories to identifying referents, suffixes, and embedded pronouns in learned and unlearned פסוקים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Pronoun referent work is highly relevant to generated-question QA and parsing.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Pronoun referent confusion is a high-value diagnostic signal in פסוק understanding.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

core_level_expectations.1: Use subjective pronouns in English and basic Hebrew
core_level_expectations.2: State referent of subjective pronoun in learned פסוק
core_level_expectations.3: State referent even when it appears in another learned פסוק
core_level_expectations.4: Identify subjective pronoun referents in learned and unlearned פסוקים
core_level_expectations.5: Continue with more pronoun types and suffixes
core_level_expectations.6: Use pronoun categories to aid translation
core_level_expectations.7: Use איתן / תהימון as pronoun clues
core_level_expectations.8: Independently identify pronoun referents and suffixes

additional_rows_summary: Distinguishes first, second, and third person.
Handles possessive, objective, demonstrative, prepositional, and interrogative pronouns.
Moves from standalone pronouns to embedded suffixes and referents in learned and unlearned פסוקים.

substandards.standard_title: Prepositions, Conjunctions and the Definite Article
substandards.student_expectation: Builds from direct recognition of conjunctions and articles to distinguishing multiple prefix functions, article types, and the two meanings of את in learned and unlearned פסוקים.

question_generation_relevance.notes: A very strong future diagnostic lane because the standards name discrete, testable forms.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Misreading prefixes and function words is a central source of Chumash translation error.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

core_level_expectations.1: Translate conjunctive ו in learned פסוקים
core_level_expectations.2: Identify and translate conjunctive ו in learned פסוקים
core_level_expectations.3: Identify and translate conjunctive ו in learned and unlearned פסוקים
core_level_expectations.4: Continue conjunctive ו recognition in learned/unlearned text
core_level_expectations.5: Use ניקוד to distinguish conjunctive ו from other vavs
core_level_expectations.6: Explain changes in the ניקוד of conjunctive ו
core_level_expectations.7: Continue identifying variants of conjunctive ו
core_level_expectations.8: Use conjunction/article/prefix knowledge accurately in unlearned פסוקים

additional_rows_summary: Distinguishes ו ההיפוך.
Handles ה הידיעה, ה השאלה, and ה המגמה.
Identifies ב-כ-ל-מ prefixes and their ניקוד shifts.
Notes that prefix ש is retained for broader sacred-text fluency.
Distinguishes the two functions of את.

substandards.student_expectation: Moves from identifying verbs and simple tense markers to translating verbs across person, tense, active/passive forms, and binyan families.

question_generation_relevance.notes: One of the richest future diagnostic/question-generation standards, but it must remain human-reviewed.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Verb parsing and tense/binyan errors are central to independent text comprehension.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

core_level_expectations.1: Identify verbs from learned words and notice future-tense איתן
core_level_expectations.2: Identify verbs in learned פסוקים and translate future forms
core_level_expectations.3: Identify verbs in unlearned פסוקים by form
core_level_expectations.4: Identify more past/present patterns by conjugation
core_level_expectations.7: Recognize passive versus active, especially נפעל
core_level_expectations.8: Translate verbs across tense, person, binyan, and suffix patterns

additional_rows_summary: Tracks future, past, present, and command forms.
Uses איתן and תהימון markers.
Handles מקור and שם הפועל.
Identifies passive versus active verbs and objective suffixes.

substandards.student_expectation: Groups a פסוק into meaningful phrases and adjusts Hebrew word order when translating into English.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Excellent future source for syntax-aware translation prompts and QA.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Grouping and word-order mistakes are highly visible in student translations.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

core_level_expectations.1: Group learned פסוקים into short phrases
core_level_expectations.2: Group when literal translation is provided but grouping is not
core_level_expectations.3: Group unlearned פסוקים by סמיכות and context
core_level_expectations.5: Adjust English word order for unlearned פסוקים
core_level_expectations.8: Independently group and reorder unlearned פסוקים

additional_rows_summary: Changes Hebrew word order appropriately when translating into English.

substandards.student_expectation: Uses טעמי המקרא to divide, punctuate, and translate a פסוק appropriately.

question_generation_relevance.notes: A strong future teacher-reviewed lane for advanced parsing tasks.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Parsing with טעמים directly affects translation and comprehension quality.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

core_level_expectations.1: Use אתנחתא and סוף פסוק to split a פסוק
core_level_expectations.2: Add זקף / סגול / רביעי for punctuation
core_level_expectations.3: Add מרכא-טפחא, קדמא-אזלא, and תביר-טפחא as linkage clues
core_level_expectations.4: Use expanded טעמים to read and punctuate
core_level_expectations.8: Use learned טעמים comprehensively for parsing

additional_rows_summary: Reads, translates, and punctuates a פסוק using increasingly rich טעמי המקרא knowledge.

substandards.student_expectation: For advanced students, explains the systems of תנועות, שבאים, דגשים, syllables, and the interaction between ניקוד and טעמי המקרא.

question_generation_relevance.notes: Highly relevant for advanced grammar and later Rashi work, but too sensitive for unreviewed question generation.

diagnostic_relevance.notes: Powerful for advanced students, but intentionally gated because the extracted table is compact and advanced.

substandards.notes: Detailed extraction appears in the Standard 3 file.

core_level_expectations.7: Differentiate types of דגשים and divide into syllables
core_level_expectations.8: Explain how טעמי המקרא affect ניקוד

additional_rows_summary: The source explicitly notes that this standard is for advanced students.
Lower-level placement should be confirmed against the PDF before any downstream use.
